# Page 4^page=4
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 3 1. Actors Acquisition App Manager A technically qualified person that creates and manages acquisition applications that serve providing POD to the system. Acquisition App User A person that uses acquisition applications to provide POD to the system. Admin A person responsible for the technical aspects of system's functioning. Data Quality Manager A person dedicated to assuring quality of POD provided by the system. Data Supplier A person responsible for providing POD, acting in the name of the given data owner. Data User Any person (citizen, enterpreneur, ...) that can use the POD provided by the system. Metadata Manager A person responsible for providing and managing definitions of datasets and data schemas. POD App Developer A technically qualified person that creates applications that use POD provided by the system. Sensor An external device providing time series data to the system. Time An abstract representation of temporal event sequence.

# Page 5^page=5
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 4 2. Use cases 2.1. Data management Figure 1 : Data management Add catalog as part of a catalog After selecting an option, a form for entering a catalog is shown by the system. After filling-in catalog data, the catalog is added as a sub-catalog to the current catalog. Add definition of dataset After selecting an option, a form for entering a dataset is shown by the system. After entering the (meta)data of the data set it is saved in the system. Edit definition of dataset After selecting a dataset, a form for editing the dataset is shown by the system. After editing the (meta)data of the data set it is saved in the system. Execute dataset transformation After selecting a dataset, a form for selecting a *door:SchemaTransformation* is shown by the system. After selecting, the system performs the transformation and creates a new dataset. Generate acquisition app After selecting datasets, a form for selecting a *door:AcquisitionApp* is shown by the system. After selecting, the system generates the app based on the selected datasets. Set data schema for dataset After selecting a dataset, a form allowing to select from available *door:DataSchema*s is shown by the system. After selecting a schemaa, it is assigned to the dataset. Show catalog with editing (as in the general use case) When the catalog window is shown, the various <invoke> use cases for editing can be started.

# Page 6^page=6
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 5 Show catalog with generation (as in the general use case) When the catalog window is shown, the various <invoke> use cases for app generation can be started. Show data-related requests After selecting an option, the system shows a window with a list of *door:DataRelatedRequest*s. Show generated apps list After selecting an option, the system shows a window with a list of apps generated using *door:AcquisitionApp*s. 2.2. Schema management Figure 2 : Schema management Add data owner After selecting an option, a form for entering a new data owner is shown by the system. After entering the information about the data owner it is saved in the system. Add data schema After selecting an option, a form for entering a new data schema is shown by the system. After entering the specification of the schema it is saved in the system. Add schema transformation After selecting an option, a form for entering a new schema transformation is shown by the system. After entering the specification of the transformation it is saved in the system. Edit data schema After selecting a data schema, a form for its editing is shown by the system. After editing the specification of the schema it is saved in the system.

# Page 7^page=7
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 6 Edit schema transformation After selecting a schema transformation, a form for its editing is shown by the system. After editing the specification of the transformation it is saved in the system. Show data owner list The user selects an option and the system retrieves the list of *door:DataOwner*s for the given user. The system finally shows a window with the list. When the window is shown, the available <invoke> use cases for editing can be started. Show data schema list The user selects an option and the system retrieves the list of *door:DataSchema*s for the given user. The system finally shows a window with the list. When the window is shown, the various <invoke> use cases for editing can be started. The user can also select an option to browse versions of the selected schema. The system then shows a list of schema versions which can be edited or added. Show schema transformation list The user selects an option and the system retrieves the list of *door:SchemaTransformation*s for the given user. The system finally shows a window with the list. When the window is shown, the various <invoke> use cases for editing can be started. 2.3. Data supply Figure 3 : Data supply

# Page 8^page=8
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 7 Add data entry to dataset After selecting an option, a form for entering a new data entry is shown by the system. After entering the data entry it is added to the dataset by the system. Edit data entry After selecting a data entry, a form for its editing is shown by the system. After editing the specification of the entry it is saved in the system. Edit data owner profile After selecting an option, the system shows a form for entering *vcard:Kind* personal data associated with the *door:DataOwner* for the current *door:DataSupplier*. This personal data is then stored in the system. Mark data entry as suspicious After selecting a data entry, a form for marking it is shown by the system. After specifying the suspicion it is added to the data entry by the system. Set quality tag for dataset After selecting a dataset, a form for entering a *door:QualityTag* it is shown by the system. After specifying the quality tag it is added to the dataset by the system. Show app dataset list The user selects an option and the system retrieves the list of *door:Dataset*s assigned to the given user. The system finally shows a window with the list. When the window is shown, the <invoke> use cases for updating the datasets can be started. Show comments on dataset After selecting a dataset, the system shows a window with a list of *door:DatasetComment*s for this dataset. Show dataset The user selects a *door:Dataset* which is then retrieved by the system and shown in a window with a list of its *door:DataEntry"s. The user can then <invoke> a use case related to a selected entry. Show dataset as quality controller (as in the general use case) When the dataset window is shown, the <invoke> use cases for quality management can be started. Show dataset as supplier (as in the general use case) When the dataset window is shown, the <invoke> use cases for updating and submitting the dataset can be started. Show dataset quality/validity alerts After selecting an option, the system shows a window with a list of *door:QualityValidityAlert*s for the given dataset. Show owned dataset list The user selects an option and the system retrieves the list of *door:Dataset*s for the given user. The system finally shows a window with the list and marked regarding comments and quality tags. When the window is shown, the <invoke> use cases for quality management can be started.

# Page 9^page=9
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 8 Show quality controllable datasets The user selects an option and the system retrieves the list of *door:Dataset*s for the given user. The system finally shows a window with the list and marked regarding comments and quality tags. When the window is shown, the <invoke> use cases for quality management can be started. Submit data entry to dataset (as in the general use case) The added data entry is immediately submitted to an already submitted dataset. Submit dataset The user selects an option and the system changes the status of the dataset as "submitted" and makes it publicly available. Upload dataset distribution After selecting an option, the system shows a form for entering information required to upload *door:DatasetDistribution*. After entering this information, the distribution is added to the selected dataset.

# Page 10^page=10
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 9 2.4. Data usage Figure 4 : Data usage Add app data specification After selecting an option, a form for entering a new app data specification is shown by the system. After entering the specification it is saved in the system. Browse data presented through a mobile/web app This is a very generic use case that represents the POD-related functionality of a mobile/web app. The various datasets can be browsed in different formats according to the required functionality, visual design and data contents. Browse data presented through a web portal This is a very generic use case that represents the POD-related functionality of a web portal. The various datasets can be browsed in different formats according to the required functionality, visual design and data contents. Download dataset After selecting a dataset, the user can select a *dcat:Distribution* for this dataset (eg. determining the file formats: JSON, CSV, XLS, ...). Then, the file of the given distribution is downloaded from the system. Edit app data specification After selecting a app data specification, a form for its editing is shown by the system. After editing the specification it is saved in the system.

# Page 11^page=11
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 10 Generate app code After selecting a *door:App DataSpecification*, the system generates the app based on the selected specification. Show app data specification list The user selects an option and the system retrieves the list of *door:AppDataSpecification*s for the given user. The system finally shows a window with the list. When the window is shown, the various <invoke> use cases for editing can be started. The user can also select an option to browse versions of the selected specification. The system then shows a list of versions which can be edited or added. Show catalog The user selects an option and the system retrieves the main *dcat:Catalog* for the given user. The system finally shows a window with a list of *dcat:Dataset* and (optionally) a list of sub- catalogs contained in the catalog. After selecting a sub-catalog, a window for the sub-catalog is shown. Show dataset description After the user selects a dataset, the system shows a window with the *dcat:Dataset* details. Show dataset preview After the user selects a dataset, the system shows a window with a simplified view of a fragment of the dataset. If the preview is not available, the system shows a respective message. Submit comment on dataset After the user selects an option, the system shows a foprm for entering a *door:DatasetComment* (eg. a request for lacking data). The comment is then attached to the current dataset. Submit data-related request After selecting an option, a form for entering a new *door:DataRelatedRequest* is shown by the system. After entering the request it is saved by the system. 2.5. Automation and administration Figure 5 : Automation and administration

# Page 12^page=12
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 11 Browse data quality/validity alerts After selecting an option, the system shows a window with a list of all current *door:QualityValidityAlert*s. Browse data service availability alerts After selecting an option, the system shows a window with a list of all current *door:AvailabilityAlert*s. Check data service availability Periodically, the system checks if the various data services associated with dataset *door:Distribution*s are responding correctly. In case of any problems a *door:AvailabilityAlert* is created. Check time series data quality/validity Periodically, the system checks if recent *door:TimeSeries* dataset entries are valid according to validity rules. In case of any problems a *door:QualityValidityAlert* is created. Deliver time series data General use case representing the functionality of external sources of time series data and their delivery to the system.

# Page 13^page=13
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 12 3. Vocabulary 3.1. Core Structure Figure 6 : Data Schemas

# Page 14^page=14
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 13 Figure 7 : Structured Datasets door:DataEntry A single record within a structured dataset, compying to the related data schema. Intended for provisioning through an acquisition app. door:DataSchema A schema that defines the structure of datasets conforming to it (classes, properties, relations). door:SchemaTransformation A definition of transformation between datasets conforming to two data schemas. A source dataset in one schema is transformed into a target dataset in another schema. door:StructuredDataset A door:QualityControllableDataset that represents data organised according to data schemas.

# Page 15^page=15
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 14 door:TimeSeries A door:QualityControllableDataset that represents data delivered and organised as time series. door:TransformationRule A rule specifying the way certain properties of the source dataset should be transformed into properties of the target dataset. door:DatasetStatus Status of the dataset in its lifecycle. 3.2. DataHandling Figure 8 : Apps

# Page 16^page=16
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 15 Figure 9 : Maintenance door:AcquisitionApp Contains a definition of an app dedicated to data acquisition, based on the associated door:DataSpecification. door:AppDataSpecification A kind of door:DataSpecification intended for generating POD usage apps. door:DataOwner A person, kind of foaf:Agent, responsible for the given dataset. Contains contact information (vcard:Kind) for reporting problems with the dataset. door:DataRelatedRequest A general request to improve offer of POD, eg. suggestions of new datasets to be provided or indications of incorrect data spanning across many datasets.

# Page 17^page=17
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 16 door:DataSpecification A set of data schemas with a definition of their provisioning and/or usage through some application. door:DataSupplier A person, kind of foaf:Agent, responsible for providing data for the given dataset, acting in the name of the given data owner. door:DatasetComment An information abut some problems associated with a given dataset provided by one of its users (eg. lacking data, incorrect data, suspicious data). 3.3. DataQuality Figure 10 : DataQuality

# Page 18^page=18
DoorCE Toolbox Requirements Page 17 QualityControllableDistribution A dcat:Distribution that can have attached quality tags. door:AvailabilityAlert An information specifying some problem with the availability (access to) a specific distribution of a dataset. door:DataFlag A record indicating some problem (suspicion or error) with a data entry. door:QualityControllableDataset A dcat:Dataset that can have attached quality tags. door:QualityTag A record indication quality of a given dataset. door:QualityValidityAlert An information specifying some infraction of rules regarding quality of validity of data in datasets. 3.4. DCAT/RDF dcat:Catalog A catalogue or repository that hosts the Datasets being described. dcat:Dataset A conceptual entity that represents the information published. dcat:Distribution A physical embodiment of the Dataset in a particular format. dct:Standard A standard or other specification to which a Dataset or Distribution conforms. foaf: Agent An entity performing some activities (person, group, software). rdfs:Resource Anything described by RDF. vcard:Kind Contains contact information. Parent for the four explicit types of vCards (Individual, Organization, Location, Group).
